metadata.name: Trembled #15
metadata.description:
Inspired by how a field of tall grasses moves and trembles when hit by the wind.

There might be an array of imaginary clouds showing up.

Press ’s’ on the live mode to save the screen capture.

Made using Hydra (https://hydra.ojack.xyz/)

Rangga Purnama Aji, 2022
https://linktr.ee/ranggapuraji

metadata.name: Zima Moon #28
metadata.description:
Developed using Hydra.js by Olivia Jack and implemented in p5.js

A beautiful constellation of pixels under moonlight, this piece recreates my perception of a forgotten night where you would spend more time watching it if you had nothing else to do.

There are 45 predefined color palettes; The movement of the work always varies according to the hash. You can print the screen by pressing 'p' in LiveMode.

metadata.name: Lysergic Paintings / Interactrip / #36
metadata.description:
Interactive piece created with hydra trough an HTML with modified functions. Each piece is a unique live generative painting based in a lot of variables, so the possibilities are just infinite! You will never see a piece like yours ♥

Plus: Each painting is also INTERACTIVE, to interact with modulations move your mouse arround the canvas! Let your mouse in the center to disable modulations and see the pure painting.

/ The modulations change too depending of the variables  composing the painting, each piece will also be affected differently by each modulation! /

Enjoy your trip 👽👽👽👽👽

I do work in an obsolete hardware that is almost dying, and i hope so much be able to buy a new pc soon! All earnings from this piece will be directed to buy a new laptop to work in my NFTS!  If you mint this  unique token, you are supporting me more than ever ♥ 

by: @temenosart

Access hydra -  hydra.ojack.xyz

metadata.name: tales of an older future #80
metadata.description:
Generative audiovisuals - live cinema meets blockchain as Russian retro futurist propaganda.

Alexandre Rangel, 2021
www.alexandrerangel.art.br

metadata.name: Palantír #186
metadata.description:
A palantír. From Quenya palan, 'far; tir, 'watch over'. The palantíri were used for communication and to see events in past or future. those using the stones had to "possess great strength of will and of mind" to direct the stone's gaze to its full capability. 

Generative livecode made on hydra.ojack.xyz / Marcelo Moura / CC0

metadata.name: Adjacent #75
metadata.description:
Adjacent is the third in a set of collections based on a month-long study of Edward Zajec, one of the early pioneers of generative art. This piece focuses on the study of Diagonal White, a series he developed between 1970 and 1975.

In 1976, Ruth Leavitt published a collection of papers titled "Artist & Computer", which highlighted the 35 prominent generative artists and asked them to discuss their work. They were posed questions like, "Do you feel art work created with a computer has now or will have an impact on art as a whole in the future?". Edward Zajec was a subject of one of those papers and in it detailed the approach to Diagonal White. 

The original program was run on a CDC 6200 computer at The Univeresiry of Trieste. The programming was done in collaboration with Dr. Matjaz Hmeljak, his long-time technical partner. Inspired by his collaborative relationship, Adjacent the output of a partnership between Christine Hall and Chris Maddern.

Controls (in live view):
- Press [s] to save a high-resolution version of the image

Made in p5js
Created January 2023
By @christine_hall and @chrismaddern

metadata.name: Tendencies #36
metadata.description:
Tendencies is the first in a set of collections based on a month-long study of Edward Zajec, one of the early pioneers of generative art in the 1970s.

Zajec's generative work was motivated by redundancy in his paintings: "The monotony of the manual task and the limited number of variations which I was able to produce in a given time made me realize the inadequacy of traditional methods in dealing with our present reality." Tendencies was inspired by — and pays tribute to — one if Zajec's earliest forays into generative creation: a work titled RAM 10 V.15, which was written on an IBM 1620 and programmed in FORTRAN.

The title of the collection itself is a reference to the first exhibition of Zajec's RAM series, while the number of editions minted referencing the year Zajec put plotter to paper.

Made in p5js
Created December 2022
By @christine_hall

The pixel will disintegrate and create a pixelated waterfall modulating eternally, experimental patch performed on open source modular synthesizers; vcvrack & hydra, click to play embeed music genereted on vcvrack patch. @dead_marcelo / 2022
